SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator

Class Coordinator

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator.Coordinator
All Implemented Interfaces:
MessageBusClientListener, ICoordinator
public class Coordinator extends Object implements ICoordinator, MessageBusClientListener
Concrete implementation of ICoordinator.

Each instance represents one node in the coordinator cluster. On startup, all nodes participate in leader election. Only the leader processes incoming API requests and publishes commands; follower nodes shadow all state updates via their message bus subscriptions so they are ready to take over instantly.

  • Constructor Details

    • Coordinator

      public Coordinator(String nodeId, List<String> peerNodeIds, String messageBusHost, int messageBusPort, int coordinatorPort)
      Constructs a Coordinator node.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - unique identifier for this node within the cluster
      peerNodeIds - IDs of all other coordinator nodes in the cluster
      messageBusHost - the hostname of the Message Bus server
      messageBusPort - the port of the Message Bus server
      coordinatorPort - the port this coordinator node listens on for API requests

  • Method Details

    • onMessage

      public void onMessage(Message<?> message)

      Enqueues all incoming messages from the Message Bus for processing by the coordinator's main logic.

      Specified by:

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts this coordinator node: registers with the message bus, subscribes to relevant topics (REGISTER, HEARTBEAT, COMMAND results), and initiates the leader election process.

      Starts the Netty request server, registers this node with the message bus, subscribes to relevant topics, and initiates leader election.

      Specified by:
      start in interface ICoordinator

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Gracefully shuts down this coordinator node. Unregisters from the message bus and notifies peers.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface ICoordinator

    • mainLoop

      public void mainLoop()

    • startElection

      public void startElection()
      Starts a new leader election by launching a LeaderElectionRunner in a dedicated thread.

      Called on startup and by HeartbeatMonitor when the leader's heartbeat timeout elapses. No-op if an election thread is already running.

      Creates a new LeaderElectionRunner with this node's ID, the peer node list, and the shared bus client, then starts it in a new thread named "leader-election-<nodeId>". Stores the thread and runner references so receiveOk(String) can forward OK signals to the active runner. If electionThread is already alive, returns immediately without starting a second election.

      Specified by:
      startElection in interface ICoordinator

    • receiveElection

      public void receiveElection(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an ELECTION message arrives from a lower-priority peer node.

      Per the Bully Algorithm, this node must:

      1. Reply with an OK message targeted at fromNodeId.
      2. Call ICoordinator.startElection() if not already running one, since this node has higher priority and should assert leadership.

      Publishes an OK message back to fromNodeId via the bus client (targeted ElectionMessage with type OK and targetNodeId = fromNodeId), then calls startElection() to assert this node's higher priority.

      Specified by:
      receiveElection in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID that sent the ELECTION message

    • receiveOk

      public void receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an OK message arrives targeted at this node.

      Signals the active LeaderElectionRunner that a higher-priority node is alive via electionRunner.receiveOk(), causing it to stand down.

      Forwards the signal to the active LeaderElectionRunner via electionRunner.receiveOk(fromNodeId) so the runner stands down after its timeout. If no election is currently running (electionRunner is null), this call is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      receiveOk in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID of the higher-priority peer that sent OK

    • receiveCoordinator

      public void receiveCoordinator(String newLeaderId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when a COORDINATOR broadcast arrives.

      Updates this node's local currentLeaderId to newLeaderId and cancels any in-progress election thread. If this node is the new leader, activates leader-only responsibilities: starts the AutomationHub thread and begins accepting requests from the API Gateway.

      Sets currentLeaderId = newLeaderId. If newLeaderId equals this node's own ID, transitions this node into leader mode. In all cases, clears electionThread and electionRunner.

      Specified by:
      receiveCoordinator in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      newLeaderId - the node ID of the newly elected leader

    • getCurrentLeaderId

      public String getCurrentLeaderId()
      Returns the node ID of the currently elected leader coordinator. All nodes — leader and follower — maintain this value. The API Gateway calls this on any coordinator node to discover where to route requests.

      Returns the value of currentLeaderId, set by the most recent receiveCoordinator(String) call. Returns null if no election has completed yet.

      Specified by:
      getCurrentLeaderId in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:
      the node ID of the current leader, or null if no election has completed yet

    • isLeader

      public boolean isLeader()
      Returns whether this coordinator instance is the current leader.

      Returns nodeId.equals(currentLeaderId).

      Specified by:
      isLeader in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:
      true if this node's ID equals currentLeaderId

    • registerUser

      public void registerUser(User user)
      Registers a new user with the system. All coordinator nodes call this so every node maintains full state.

      Persists the user in the StateStore. All coordinator nodes call this so every node maintains full state.

      Specified by:
      registerUser in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      user - the user to register

      public List<User> getAllUsers()
      Returns all users currently registered with the system.

      Returns:

      public User getUserById(String userId)
      Returns a specific registered user by its ID.
      Specified by:
      getUserById in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      userId - the ID of the user to look up
      Returns:
      the User with the given ID, or null if not found

    • registerDevice

      public void registerDevice(RegistrationPayload payload)
      Handles a device registration event received from the REGISTER topic. Creates a Device record and stores it in the db. All coordinator nodes call this so every node maintains full state.

      Called when a RegistrationPayload arrives on the REGISTER topic. Behaviour depends on whether the device is already in the store:

      • Device is claimed (user registered it first) — update the existing record with the info from the payload. The userId and name set by the user are preserved.
      • Device is unclaimed (device connected before user claimed it) — save w/ userId same as deviceId for placeholder. The record will be updated with a userId and name when the user later calls claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest).
      Specified by:
      registerDevice in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      payload - the info about the device that has registered itself

    • claimDevice

      public boolean claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest claimRequest)
      Records a user's claim on a device before the physical device has connected.

      Creates a pending device record in the state store via StateStore.claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest) with the userId and name provided in the claim request, but with with online=false and no actions. When the device subsequently boots and sends its RegistrationPayload, ICoordinator.registerDevice(RegistrationPayload) finds the existing record and merges the capabilities in rather than creating a new unclaimed entry.

      If the device has already connected (registered before being claimed), this method updates the existing record with the userId and name instead of inserting a duplicate.

      Called by the API Gateway when a user submits POST /devices/claim with a device ID, name, and type.

      Validates that the userId exists in the state store, then calls StateStore.claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest) to create or update the device record.

      Specified by:
      claimDevice in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      claimRequest - the claim request containing user ID, device ID, name, and type
      Returns:
      true if the claim succeeded, or false if the userId is invalid

    • getAllDevices

      public List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Returns all devices currently registered with the system.
      Specified by:
      getAllDevices in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:

    • handleCommand

      public void handleCommand(Command command)
      Processes a user-initiated command request. Validates the device and action, publishes the command to the COMMAND topic on the message bus, and returns the resulting status once the device responds.

      Only the current leader should call this method.

      Validates that the target device exists and supports the requested action before publishing. Unblocks once command is sent. Result is handled asynchronously when the device responds.

      Specified by:
      handleCommand in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      command - the command to dispatch

    • handleDeviceHeartbeat

      public void handleDeviceHeartbeat(String deviceId)
      Processes a heartbeat message received from an edge device. Hands over to heartbeat monitor to update the device's last-seen timestamp.
      Specified by:
      handleDeviceHeartbeat in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device that sent the heartbeat

    • handleNodeHeartbeat

      public void handleNodeHeartbeat(String nodeId)
      Processes a heartbeat received from another coordinator node. Hands over to heartbeat monitor to update the node's last-seen timestamp.
      Specified by:
      handleNodeHeartbeat in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the coordinator node that sent the heartbeat

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Entry point for a standalone Coordinator node. Usage: java Coordinator nodeId peerNodeIds_csv busHost busPort coordPort
